Application Process

Apply for Health Insurance in Minutes

The entire application is completed online. No branch visits. No paperwork. Apply for comprehensive family coverage in 4 clear steps.

1

Personal Details

Provide your personal information including name, contact details, and family members to be covered.

2

Coverage Selection

Choose your desired coverage amount and select additional benefits like maternity or dental care.

3

Upload Documents

Submit your ID documents and any medical history forms required for your health insurance application.

4

Review & Pay

Confirm your details, review the premium, make payment, and activate your health insurance policy instantly.

Note: Some treatments are subject to waiting periods, and inpatient services may require pre-authorization.

Pricing

Build a J-Junior Quote

Choose a plan, add the benefits you need, and see a real-time premium breakdown based on the current J-Junior rate card. The quote stays structured, fast, and easy to follow.

Choose cover and members

Build your quote in three quick steps

1

Select a plan

Higher plans offer higher annual limits and additional benefits.

2

Select coverage components

Inpatient stays included. Add outpatient first to unlock dental and optical.

3

Set insured members

Premiums are priced per insured member, so totals adjust as counts change.

Children At least one child is required for a quote.
Other dependents Add extra dependents if they should be covered on the same quote.
Total insured members 1

  • Certain hospitals or extended coverage may affect pricing.

Who Can Apply

Who Can Apply for J-Junior

Ugandan residents â€” citizens and legal residents

Individuals and families seeking comprehensive health coverage

People with valid ID and contact information

Children and dependents included under family plans

Coverage for pre-existing, chronic, and congenital conditions is available subject to full disclosure and applicable waiting periods.

Start Application
Before You Apply

Keep These Ready

Have these documents ready for a smooth application process.

1
Valid Identification

National ID, passport, or driving license for all members to be covered.

National ID Passport Driving License
2
Medical History Details (If Applicable)

Information about any pre-existing, chronic, or congenital conditions that need to be disclosed.

3
Contact Information

Valid phone number and email address for all covered members.

Claims Process

Simple and Guided Claims Experience

Filing a health claim is straightforward. Our team guides you through every step.

How Claims Work

4 steps from incident to settlement

1
Report the Claim

Notify us as soon as possible to begin the claims process.

2
Submit Documents

Provide required documents including medical reports, receipts, and supporting evidence.

3
Assessment

Our team reviews and verifies your claim.

4
Settlement

Approved claims are processed and paid according to your policy terms.

What You'll Need for Claims

Have these ready to avoid delays in your claim

Your medical card
Medical reports and receipts
Prescription documents
Pre-authorization letter (for inpatient claims)

Important: Inpatient treatment requires pre-authorization. Members must present their medical card and complete required claim forms.

Important Timelines

Waiting Periods

Be aware of these important timelines before using certain benefits.

General Illness

30 days from policy start

Pre-existing & Chronic Conditions

1 year from policy start

Cancer Treatment

2 years from policy start

Congenital & Inherited Conditions

1 year from policy start

Waiting periods begin from your policy effective date. After waiting periods are satisfied, covered conditions are fully eligible for benefits.

What Is Not Covered

Exclusions

These services are not covered under the policy.

Cosmetic Procedures

Unless due to accidental injury.

Alternative Treatments

Herbal medicine, acupuncture, chiropractic care.

Hazardous Activities

Injuries from extreme or hazardous activities.

Fertility Services

Treatments, family planning, and related services.

Weight Loss Treatments

Obesity-related programs and treatments.

Intentional Self-Harm

Or substance abuse and medical non-compliance.

For a comprehensive list of exclusions, refer to the policy document provided upon enrollment.

After You Apply

Enrollment & Policy Documents

What happens after your application is submitted and approved.

Application Review

1

Applications reviewed within 3 working days of submission.

2

Once approved and payment confirmed, your policy is activated immediately.

You Will Receive

Membership Card

Digital and physical cards for identification at providers.

Policy Document

Complete policy terms, conditions, and coverage details.

Provider Network List

Countrywide list of hospitals, clinics, and specialists.

Got Questions?

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know before starting your SME insurance application.

How long does the application take?+
Around 5â€“10 minutes if your documents are ready. The process is fully online with step-by-step guidance â€” no branch visits required.
Is KYC mandatory?+
Yes, KYC verification is required for policy approval. It ensures correct policy ownership, prevents fraud, and is mandatory for claims processing.
Can I insure only specific assets?+
Yes, you can choose exactly which benefits to cover. Jubilee J-Care lets you customize your health coverage based on your needs and budget.
When does coverage start?+
Coverage begins after successful payment and policy approval. You will receive your policy documents confirming your active coverage.
Can I update my policy later?+
Yes, your policy can be updated to reflect changes in your business â€” such as new assets, increased coverage limits, or changes in business scope.
How are claims processed?+
Submit your documents and proof of loss, our team verifies and assesses the claim, and settlement is made promptly in line with your policy terms.
